title: buildah push can not push to transport atomic
body:
```
[root@fedora ~]# buildah push docker.io/library/busybox:latest atomic:busybox:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:daacdb2949be2c830e78b878ff144e38132faee2c2dcc20a544a5e0460fbdbf3
 0 B / 698.35 KiB [------------------------------------------------------------]
ERRO[0005] error pushing image "docker.io/library/busybox:latest" to "atomic:busybox:latest": error copying layers and metadata: Error writing blob: Error initiating layer upload to /v2/library/busybox/blobs/uploads/, status 401 
```

comments.body:
As a guess, are you logged into the cluster (`oc login`) _and_ into the docker/distribution registry (`docker login`)?

If that's not it, a full logrus log at the debug level would be useful.

There is `.docker/config.json` exists, so I move the file outside the dir and docker logout, it still get the same error, full logrus log like below:

[root@fedora ~]# docker logout

@gouyang Thanks for the log, that's helpful.  I'm still not clear if you're logged into the cluster with "oc login".  Can you confirm that?  The issue isn't with docker, it's with the spot that you're pushing to the cluster.
FWIW, I'm currently working on putting authentication in play for 'buildah from' and after that wraps for 'buildah push'.  If oc login doesn't cure the issue for you, hopefully that work will.

> so I move the file outside the dir and docker logout

Sorry, I meant that you _should_ be logged in, _both_ via `docker login` and `oc login`.

Anyway, the primary issue in here is that `atomic:busybox:latest` means the same thing as `atomic:docker.io/library/busybox:latest`, not `busybox:latest` in your default namespace. Try using `atomic:`_$hostname_`/`_$namespace_`/`_$stream_`:`_$tag_ .

```
[root@fedora buildah-ansible]# docker login
Username: gouyang@redhat.com
Password: 
Login Succeeded

> ```
> buildah push docker.io/library/nginx:latest atomic:docker.io/library/nginx:latest
> ```

Fine, that’s the explicit syntax, but do you _really_ have permission to push to the Docker Hub library repositories? And the Docker Hub is not an OpenShift registry at all.

Use the hostname and namespace of _your OpenShift cluster_; or if that is not applicable, please describe in words what is the intended destination.

A new question is pushing to transport `atomic` equals push to Docker hub registry or OpenShift registry, not local storage, then why we use `atomic` as the transport?
atomic == OpenShift registry.  Formally called the atomic registry.
Perhaps we should get a more generic name then atomic.  @mtrmac Doesn't it really mean a registry that supports storing simple signing signatures?

> ```
> # buildah push docker.io/library/busybox:latest atomic:docker.io/gouyang/busybox:v1
> ```

`atomic`: means “OpenShift”; if it works for `docker.io`, that’s unsupported and only by accident. Can you back up and explain what is it you are primarily working on or trying to do?

> Perhaps we should get a more generic name then atomic. @mtrmac Doesn't it really mean a registry that supports storing simple signing signatures?

It specifically means a registry using the OpenShift-specific APi.  Now that we have `X-Registry-Supports-Signatures` in OpenShift, which is not OpenShift-specific (i.e. can be implemented by any other registry), does not require any OpenShift login, and can be auto-detected, most users should use `docker:` for OpenShift as well, and `atomic` should eventually be deprecated. Renaming a soon-to-be-deprecated transport is probably not worth it.
Well lets take atomic out of the documentation now, if it is not needed, and remove it from the list of alltransports.

> atomic: means “OpenShift”; if it works for docker.io, that’s unsupported and only by accident. Can you back up and explain what is it you are primarily working on or trying to do?

I think it current works for any registry if docker login is successful for that registry. 
At first, I think after `buildah push xxxx atomic:xxxx`, I can see the images by `atomic images list`, but it's not, it need to do `docker pull xxxx`, so I ask a more question above.

 

> Well lets take atomic out of the documentation now, if it is not needed, and remove it from the list of alltransports.

I think it's good that if it can push to any registry, we can leave it there and rename it to `registry` or a more proper name.
I have opened pull requests to remove/deprecate  "atomic" as a transport, and just have people use the docker transport.

> >atomic: means “OpenShift”; if it works for docker.io, that’s unsupported and only by accident. Can you back up and explain what is it you are primarily working on or trying to do?
>
> I think it current works for any registry if docker login is successful for that registry.

No it doesn’t; it happened to work for you because you are pushing without writing signatures; writing signatures or any kind of read access would fail.

Again, _what is it you are trying to do in the first place_? Let us help you.
